title: Some users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message
impact: Users intermittently may have been unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and received an error message.

updates.content:
Title: We're looking into a potential problem impacting Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: We're checking for potential impact to your users.

Current status: We're investigating a potential issue with Microsoft 365 Copilot and checking for impact to your organization. We'll provide an update within 30 minutes.

Title: Some users may experience intermittent failures with requests in Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Some users may experience intermittent failures with requests in Copilot.

More info: User may receive an error such as "sorry, something went wrong".

Current status: We're using our service-side telemetry to better understand the potential root cause and next steps.

Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot.

Next update by: Wednesday, April 15, 2026, at 7:00 PM UTC

Title: Some users may experience intermittent failures with requests in Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Some users may experience intermittent failures with requests in Copilot.

More info: User may receive an error such as "sorry, something went wrong".

Current status: We've identified a memory-related issue on a portion of infrastructure that supports Copilot requests that is likely contributing to the impact. To confirm this cause, we're applying a fix for the memory-related issue, and, in parallel, we'll continue our investigation to understand if there are other factors that may be contributing.

Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot.

Next update by: Thursday, April 16, 2026, at 7:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users may experience intermittent failures with requests in Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Some users may experience intermittent failures with requests in Copilot.

More info: User may receive an error such as "sorry, something went wrong".

Current status: Our mitigation efforts to add additional resources to support flows to the impacted infrastructure to help restore the service to a healthy reliability again, are ongoing. In tandem, our investigation into the correlation between the infrastructure and this issue continues as we determine the next steps in our plan to fully resolve this impact.

Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot.

Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC

Title: Some users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message

User impact: Users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message.

More info: User may receive an error that reads:  "sorry, something went wrong".
 
Current status: We’re continuing to add additional resources to support traffic flow to the impacted infrastructure and anticipate this action will be completed by our next scheduled communications update. Once this action is completed, we’ll be able to pinpoint what supplementary fixes are also necessary to fully resolve the issue, which will also aid in confirming the root cause of the impact.
 
Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.
 
Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 5:00 AM UTC

Title: Some users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message

User impact: Users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message.

More info: User may receive an error that reads:  "sorry, something went wrong".

Current status: We're finalizing the process of scaling up service resources supporting the affected traffic flow and are monitoring service health metrics to confirm the result and inform our next steps.

Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 9:00 AM UTC

Title: Some users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message

User impact: Users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message.

More info: User may receive an error that reads:  "sorry, something went wrong".

Current status: Our effort to scale up service resources is continuing but taking longer than expected. In parallel, we’re validating an additional fix to provide further long-term service stability.

Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 1:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message

User impact: Users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message.

More info: User may receive an error that reads:  "sorry, something went wrong".

Current status: Service resources are continuing to be scaled up, and we've deployed a fix to address underlying memory handling and instability, helping reduce further impact as deployment progresses. We expect to be able to provide a remediation timeline by our next scheduled update.

Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 5:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message

User impact: Users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message.

More info: Users may receive an error that reads:  "sorry, something went wrong".

Current status: We've determined that sustained pressure on a portion of infrastructure that handles Microsoft 365 Copilot queries is causing decreased service availability and resulting in impact. We're continuing to scale up service resources to alleviate impact while deploying our fix aimed at addressing the underlying memory handling and instability issues. We'll provide a timeline on this deployment process and impact remediation as one becomes available.

Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Monday, April 13, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC

Root cause: Sustained pressure on a portion of infrastructure that handles Microsoft 365 Copilot queries is causing decreased service availability and resulting in impact.

Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 7:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message

User impact: Users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message.

More info: Users may receive an error that reads: "sorry, something went wrong."

Current status: We're continuing to monitor our fix deployment as it saturates throughout the affected infrastructure and aim to provide a timeline on this process and overall impact remediation as one becomes available. Meanwhile, we've confirmed that scaling up service resources is successfully alleviating impact while this deployment process is ongoing.

Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Monday, April 13, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC

Root cause: Sustained pressure on a portion of infrastructure that handles Microsoft 365 Copilot queries is causing decreased service availability and resulting in impact.

Next update by: Friday, April 17, 2026, at 9:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message

User impact: Users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message.

More info: Users may receive an error that reads: "sorry, something went wrong."
 
Current status: Our deployment monitoring is ongoing as our developed fix continues to saturate throughout the affected infrastructure. We'll aim to provide a timeline for this saturation process and full impact remediation by our next scheduled update.

Scope of impact: This issue intermittently affects some users making requests in Copilot. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Monday, April 13, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC

Root cause: Sustained pressure on a portion of infrastructure that handles Microsoft 365 Copilot queries is causing decreased service availability and resulting in impact.

Next update by: Monday, April 20, 2026, at 9:00 PM UTC

Title: Some users intermittently may be unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and receive an error message

User impact: Users intermittently may have been unable to run queries in Microsoft 365 Copilot and received an error message.

More info: Users may have received an error that read: "sorry, something went wrong."

Final status: Our deployed fix completed saturation and we've confirmed through service health monitoring that the impact is resolved.

Scope of impact: Any user running queries in Copilot may have been impacted.

Start time: Monday, April 13, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC

End time: Sunday, April 19, 2026, at 3:00 AM UTC

Root cause: Sustained pressure on a portion of infrastructure that handles Microsoft 365 Copilot queries was causing decreased service availability and caused impact.

Next steps: 
 - We're reviewing the affected infrastructure to identify the source of the sustained pressure, so that we can determine how to prevent similar impact to Copilot queries in the future.

This is the final update for the event.
